REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, 2RENEWED NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE VOLUNTARY DISMISSAL AND REQUEST TO RESTORE MOTION TO CALENDAR IS DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT THE ATTORNEY NEGLECT WAS EXCUSABLE FOR THE REASON THAT EVEN IF COUNSEL HAD INSTRUCTED HER ASSISTANT TO ATTACH A COPY OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO THE DISMISSAL, THAT STILL WOULD NOT HAVE BEEN ENOUGH FOR THE COURT TO RETAIN JURISDICTION. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT THE APPLICATION FOR RELIEF WAS MADE MORE THAN 6 MONTHS AGO. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Renewed Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Apr-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO 2renewed Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement is DENIED. The Court does not have jurisdiction to enforce the settlement.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

2renewed Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar
Off Calendar Apr-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO 2renewed Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar is DENIED. The court does not find that the attorney neglect was excusable for the reason that even if counsel had instructed her assistant to attach a copy of the settlement agreement to the dismissal, that still would not have been enough for the court to retain jurisdiction. The court further notes that the application for relief was made more than 6 months ago.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Renewed Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement; Memorandum In Support; Declaration
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Sydney Hall; 650-342-1830; Law Office of Sydney Hall; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Jeffrey Ta; 628-222-5870; LaGasse Branch Bell + Kinkead LLP; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Hall contests the courts tentative ruling and requests the matter be continued to file supplemental declarations. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO Renewed Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement; Memorandum In Support; Declaration is DENIED. Dismissal on file.

Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar
Off Calendar Mar-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Sydney Hall; 650-342-1830; Law Office of Sydney Hall; counsel for plaintiff appearing via Zoom.
Jeffrey Ta; 628-222-5870; LaGasse Branch Bell + Kinkead LLP; counsel for defendant appearing via Zoom.

Mr. Hall contests the courts tentative ruling and requests the matter be continued to file supplemental declarations. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Voluntary Dismissal And Request To Restore Motion To Calendar is DENIED without prejudice. Declaration accompanying the motion does not provide sufficient facts to support a finding of mistake, surprise or excusable neglect giving rise to discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). Declaration is devoid of any facts leading up to the dismissal or why the dismissal was filed.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROSA FRANCO MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. POS filed on October 14, 2024 attests to purported service on "Side Inc i/c/o Mcleod" as 580 4th Street. There is no proof of service for any other defendant and this address is not an address of record for any of the defendants. Furthermore, there is no proper Notice of Motion on file. The document so titled contains a declaration and no actual Notice.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion To Set Aside Dismissal
